summary: Sprint 6 as written is likely to fail because it omits a required service rebuild/redeploy, has an unverified import path for the new canonical advisory lock helper, and doesn’t include any staging data/backward-compat verification for the domain-tag hash change.

blocking_issues.issue: Plan rebuilds/redeploys only `finn` and `freeside-api`, but product context states three microservices (Freeside API, Finn, Dixie BFF/reputation) and the shared protocol library is used across the platform.
blocking_issues.why_blocking: If Dixie depends (directly or transitively) on `@0xhoneyjar/loa-hounfour`, it will continue running the old domain-tag behavior and/or old advisory lock key implementation, causing inconsistent protocol behavior across services and potentially breaking cross-service verification or reputation/audit flows. This directly violates the sprint acceptance criterion “Docker images rebuilt…ECS services redeployed and healthy” for the platform upgrade.
blocking_issues.fix: Add explicit dependency audit + deployment scope task: (1) `npm ls @0xhoneyjar/loa-hounfour` per service/package (including Dixie) to confirm consumers; (2) if Dixie consumes it, add Docker build/tag/push for Dixie ECR repo and force-new-deployment for `arrakis-staging-dixie` (or document and prove Dixie is not a consumer with a verifiable check). Update AC to list exactly which services are rebuilt/redeployed and why.

blocking_issues.location: Sprint 6 — Task 6.2 (replace advisoryLockKey with canonical export)
blocking_issues.issue: Import path `@0xhoneyjar/loa-hounfour/commons/advisory-lock-key` is assumed but not validated; monorepo uses GitHub dependency + postinstall dist rebuild, so subpath exports can differ between source and built dist/package.json exports map.
blocking_issues.why_blocking: If the package does not export that subpath in its published/built `dist` (or uses an `exports` map that blocks deep imports), TypeScript/Node resolution will fail at build/test time, stopping the sprint. This is a common failure mode with GitHub-pinned deps + custom dist rebuild scripts.
blocking_issues.fix: Add a task to verify the actual exported entrypoint in v8.3.1: check `node_modules/@0xhoneyjar/loa-hounfour/package.json` `exports` and the built file layout after `npm install`. If deep import is not supported, switch to the supported barrel export (e.g., `@0xhoneyjar/loa-hounfour/commons` or top-level export) and/or add a protocol task to update hounfour to re-export `computeAdvisoryLockKey` from a stable path. Update AC to require `tsc -b` (or service build) passes, not just unit tests.

blocking_issues.issue: Domain tag sanitization changes hash inputs (dots→hyphens) and the plan only updates one conformance vector (V-A02) and runs tests; it does not include any verification against existing stored audit trail data in staging (or an explicit epoch-boundary compatibility check).
blocking_issues.why_blocking: Audit trail integrity is a critical requirement; changing domain tags can change hash chain values. Unit/conformance tests can pass while staging data verification fails (e.g., inability to verify existing chains, mismatched anchors, or mixed-format tags). If this happens after deploy, the sprint fails the “audit trail integrity (hash chains must be verifiable)” requirement even if CI is green.
blocking_issues.fix: Add an explicit staging backward-compat verification task before deploy: run a script/job that (a) selects a sample of existing audit trail entries created pre-upgrade, (b) verifies their hash chain/anchors using the old epoch rules, and (c) verifies new entries created post-upgrade verify under the new tag format without breaking the chain rules (or confirms epoch boundary separation). Make the AC measurable (e.g., “verify N=100 historical trails + N=10 newly created trails; 0 verification failures”).

blocking_issues.location: Sprint 6 — Task 6.1 (pinning to GitHub tag v8.3.1)
blocking_issues.issue: Pinning to `github:0xHoneyJar/loa-hounfour#v8.3.1` assumes the tag is immutable and that npm will resolve it consistently; the plan doesn’t require lockfile update/verification or a clean install check.
blocking_issues.why_blocking: In GitHub dependencies, tag movement or lockfile drift can lead to different commits being installed across environments, making “version 8.3.1” checks misleading (package.json version can remain 8.3.1 even if code differs). This can cause non-reproducible builds and failed deploy verification.
blocking_issues.fix: Add explicit lockfile and commit verification: require `package-lock.json` (or npm lockfile) updated and committed; verify `npm ci` from a clean workspace installs the expected commit (record resolved `resolved` URL / commit SHA from lockfile). Optionally pin by commit SHA for reproducibility and document why tag pin is acceptable if you keep it.

blocking_issues.issue: Redeploy step only says “force new deployment” and checks running=1; it doesn’t include updating task definition image digests/tags or verifying the new image is actually running (ECS can redeploy the same image tag if not updated).
blocking_issues.why_blocking: If the task definition still points at an old image digest (or `staging` tag is cached/unchanged), the service may come up healthy but still run the old hounfour version, failing the upgrade objective while appearing successful.
blocking_issues.fix: Add explicit task-definition revision update: register new task definition revision with the new image digest/tag (prefer immutable SHA tag), update service to that revision, then verify running task image digest via `describe-tasks` (container image) and/or application startup log includes hounfour commit/tag. Update AC to require task definition revision changed and running tasks reference the new image digest.
